rm_Demo_Description: What you’ll learn in this interactive walkthrough This side-by-side chat demo introduces vLLM and shows how compressed large language models can deliver significant performance and efficiency gains. rm_Sales_Tactic: Mission Critical Automation - Maximize technology investments (AAP + AI/RHEL/Virt/ISV)
rm_Verticals: No, it is applicable to multiple verticals
rm_Demo_Description: Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL) is an essential foundation for IT operations and must be managed to stay up to date. This is where Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform comes in to manage Red Hat Enterprise Linux systems at scale. Red Hat Ansible is a highly flexible platform that can manage the full operational lifecycle of RHEL systems and related infrastructure. Learn the details in this interactive overview.

rm_Final_Demo_Title: Network automation with Red Hat Ansible
rm_Product: Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform, N/A
rm_Sales_Tactic: Mission Critical Automation - Network automation
rm_Verticals: No, it is applicable to multiple verticals
rm_Demo_Description: Click through for a high-level overview of Network automation (hyperlink: https://www.redhat.com/en/technologies/management/ansible/network-automation) with Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform. The tour covers: - Discovering what can be automated in your network and the benefits of Ansible Certified Content Collections (hyperlink: https://catalog.redhat.com/platform/red-hat-ansible) - Describing how network automation can meet your challenges - Identifying 3 main network automation use case categories: network visibility, configuration management, and network operations - Generating a network visibility report by using the new Validated Network Report feature (via one integrated demo example) - Understanding how Southwest Airlines (hyperlink: https://www.redhat.com/en/resources/southwest-airlines-customer-story) saved significant time on network management, while reducing risk and accelerating innovation with Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform

rm_Final_Demo_Title: Self-service provisioning ephemeral environments with Red Hat Developer Hub and ACM (Platform Engineering)
rm_Product: Red Hat Developer Hub, Red Hat OpenShift, Red Hat OpenShift Virtualization, Advanced Cluster Management for Kubernetes
rm_Sales_Tactic: Container Management - Multicluster management and security at scale for Kubernetes.
rm_Verticals: No, it is applicable to multiple verticals
rm_Demo_Description: Gartner named Platform Engineering one of the top 10 strategic technology trends for 2024 and predicts that by 2026. Self-service is one of the core pillars of Platform Engineering. Click through for a high-level overview showcasing how Red Hat Developer Hub can be self-service, bringing capabilities from OpenShift, ACS, ACM, and third-party tools. Self-service provisioning ephemeral environments with Red Hat Developer Hub and ACM (Platform Engineering) In this section, you will learn: - Fundamentals of Red Hat Developer Hub (RHDH) - Overview of Software templates - Self-service with Red Hat Developer Hub - Overview Red Hat Red Hat Advanced Cluster Management for Kubernetes - Overview of Hosted Control Planes and OpenShift Virtualization - How to build ephemeral environments with RHDH

rm_Final_Demo_Title: Self-service provisioning VMs with Red Hat Developer Hub (Platform Engineering)
rm_Product: Red Hat Developer Hub, Red Hat OpenShift, Red Hat OpenShift Virtualization
rm_Sales_Tactic: Application Platform - Modernize legacy apps & develop net-new cloud-native apps
rm_Verticals: No, it is applicable to multiple verticals
rm_Demo_Description: Red Hat Developer Hub and OpenShift Virtualization Red Hat Developer Hub provides a single pane of glass that also allows legacy applications and third-party systems to be migrated by leveraging Red Hat OpenShift Virtualization. Teams can build virtual machines through the Red Hat Developer Hub, streamlining the development process and reducing complexity. This comprehensive approach not only enhances productivity but also supports the seamless integration of diverse application delivery technologies. Benefits of building virtual machines with Red Hat Developer Hub include: - The ability to scale operations by providing self-service provisioning. - Consistency across organizations based on best practices when building in VMs. - The capability to ensure the VMs created are the only ones available for provisioning. - The capacity to automate VM creation using software templates for consistency and scale across different teams.
